rulings.Case Title: JACK OLSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and punitive damages and off calendar as to strict liability. As to negligence, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Olson was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Mr. Olson¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s interrogatory responses included cites to and summarizes of various documents and evidence they alleged supported their contentions against defendant, and defendant¿s separate statement failed to discuss those items or demonstrate that they do not support plaintiffs¿ contentions. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Defendant¿s moving papers failed to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages, or that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto. Therefore defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar as to punitive damages. Plaintiffs have dismissed their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:00am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Navarro was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant because Mr. Navarro, Jr.¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories (regarding Cranite) preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However, defendant sustained its initial burden as to substantial factor causation through its material facts nos. 14 and 15 and plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue whether any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to decedent¿s risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:00am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
